The user is asking for a biography of Jack Leach. This likely refers to the English cricketer. Let me search for information about him.Here is a biography of **Jack Leach**, the English cricketer:

---

## Jack Leach 芒聙聯 Biography

**Matthew Jack Leach** (born 22 June 1991) is an English professional cricketer who plays internationally for the England Test cricket team. In domestic cricket, he represents **Somerset County Cricket Club**. A left-arm orthodox spin bowler, he is known by the nicknames "Leachy" and "The Nut."

### Early Life and Education

Leach was born in **Taunton, Somerset, England**. He attended Trinity School, Bishop Fox's School, and Richard Huish College, all in Taunton. Before his cricket career took off, he worked parking trolleys at a Sainsbury's supermarket in Taunton. At the age of **14**, he was diagnosed with **Crohn's disease**, a condition he has managed throughout his career. He completed a degree in sports coaching at the University of Wales Institute, Cardiff (Cardiff Metropolitan University).

### Domestic Career

Leach graduated from the **Somerset County Cricket Club academy** in the summer of 2010 and signed a professional contract. He first represented **Dorset County Cricket Club** in the 2010 Minor Counties Cricket Championship, bowling his side to victory in the final with figures of 6/21.

He made his first-class debut for Somerset in August 2012 against Lancashire. His maiden five-wicket haul came in 2013 against Warwickshire, where he bowled 44 overs (24 of them maidens). He spent the 2012芒聙聯13 winter playing grade cricket for Valley District Cricket Club in Brisbane, Australia.

In April 2022, he was bought by the **Birmingham Phoenix** for The Hundred. In June 2024, he signed a new two-year contract with Somerset.

### International Career

#### Debut and Early Years (2018芒聙聯2021)

Leach was called up to England's Test squad in March **2018** as an injury replacement for Mason Crane during the tour of New Zealand. He made his debut in the second Test in **Christchurch** (cap #684).

His most iconic moment came during the **2019 Ashes** at Headingley. In one of the most dramatic finishes in Test history, Leach scored **1 not out** in a partnership of 76 with **Ben Stokes**, helping England win by one wicket. His single run has been described as "arguably the greatest one not out in the history of the game." His glasses-wearing persona and on-field mannerisms earned him a cult following, and he even received free Specsavers glasses for life.

In the one-off Test against Ireland at Lord's in 2019, Leach scored **92 runs** as a nightwatchman, earning him Man of the Match honors in England's 143-run victory.

#### First-Choice Spinner (2021芒聙聯Present)

Under the captaincy of **Ben Stokes**, Leach became England's first-choice spinner. He played every Test for which he was available and ended **2022** as the **third-highest wicket-taker in the world** in Test cricket (behind only Kagiso Rabada and Nathan Lyon). His first ten-wicket haul in Tests came during the 2022 series against New Zealand, with figures of 10/166.

However, injuries plagued him: he missed the 2023 Ashes with a **lower back stress fracture**, and in January 2024, a **knee injury** during the India tour ruled him out of the remainder of that series. His Somerset teammate **Shoaib Bashir** was subsequently preferred for the West Indies series.

In **October 2024**, Leach returned to the England team for the Pakistan tour, taking seven wickets in the first Test including 4/30 in the second innings, helping England to a record-breaking victory.

### Career Statistics (as of April 2026)

| | Tests | First-Class | List A | T20 |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Matches** | 39 | 168 | 33 | 9 |
| **Wickets** | 142 | 551 | 45 | 14 |
| **Bowling Average** | 34.07 | 27.76 | 27.77 | 17.85 |
| **Best Bowling** | 5/66 | 8/85 | 6/26 | 3/28 |
| **5-Wicket Hauls** | 5 | 34 | 1 | 0 |
| **Top Score** | 92 | 92 | 18 | 8* |

*Source: ESPNcricinfo*

---

FINAL ANSWER: Jack Leach (Matthew Jack Leach, born 22 June 1991 in Taunton, Somerset) is an English professional cricketer 芒聙聰 a left-arm orthodox spin bowler who plays Test cricket for England and domestic cricket for Somerset. Diagnosed with Crohn's disease at age 14, he graduated from Somerset's academy in 2010 and made his Test debut in 2018 against New Zealand. He became a cult hero during the 2019 Ashes at Headingley, where his 1 not out partnership with Ben Stokes secured a famous one-wicket win. He also scored 92 as a nightwatchman against Ireland at Lord's. Under Ben Stokes's captaincy, he became England's first-choice spinner, finishing 2022 as the world's third-highest Test wicket-taker. Injuries (back stress fracture in 2023, knee injury in 2024) sidelined him at times, but he returned in October 2024 to help England to a record-breaking win in Pakistan. As of April 2026, he has 142 Test wickets and 551 first-class wickets.
